parameters.query:
SELECT app_name, package_name, install_method
FROM package_dictionary
WHERE distro = '{{ $json.base_distro }}'
  AND app_name = ANY(ARRAY[{{ $json.apps.map(a => `'${a}'`).join(',') }}]::text[])
ORDER BY app_name;

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ status: 'enterprise_initiated', userId: $('Build Dispatch Payload').item.json.userId, base_distro: $('Build Dispatch Payload').item.json.base_distro, docker_image: $('Build Dispatch Payload').item.json.docker_image, package_count: $('Build Dispatch Payload').item.json.package_count, resolved_at: $('Build Dispatch Payload').item.json.resolved_at, message: 'Enterprise build dispatched. Your ISO will be ready in ~15 minutes.' }) }}

_importInstructions.cred_1: Supabase PostgreSQL — host: db.<your-project>.supabase.co, port: 5432, db: postgres, user: postgres, password: <your-supabase-db-password>
_importInstructions.cred_2: GitHub PAT — paste your github_pat_... token
_importInstructions.step2_supabase: Make sure you have run database/schema.sql in your Supabase SQL editor first.
